title: Insecure Deserialization
principle: Never deserialize untrusted bytes into live objects. Formats that reconstruct arbitrary types — Python pickle, Java's ObjectInputStream, PHP unserialize, Ruby Marshal — can execute code during deserialization. Use a data-only format (JSON) parsed into a known schema, and if a rich format is unavoidable, restrict it to an explicit allowlist of classes.

snippets.library: json (or a schema validator) instead of pickle
snippets.secure: obj = json.loads(request.data)   # data only; validate against a schema (pydantic/jsonschema)
snippets.note: pickle.loads runs __reduce__ and can execute arbitrary code. Same for PyYAML's yaml.load — use yaml.safe_load. Never unpickle data crossing a trust boundary.

snippets.library: Jackson/Gson (no default typing), or an ObjectInputFilter allowlist
snippets.note: readObject on untrusted data is the classic RCE gadget-chain sink. Never enable Jackson's default typing (enableDefaultTyping / @JsonTypeInfo) on untrusted input.
unserialize triggers magic methods (__wakeup/__destruct) that drive PHP object-injection chains. json_decode returns plain data with no such side effects.
JSON.parse cannot instantiate types or run code. Packages like node-serialize/funcster and any eval-based parsing execute embedded functions — don't feed them untrusted input.
